data.title: Corteva downgraded by Citi as spinoff adds uncertainty to 2026 outlook
data.description:
Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) on Thursday was downgraded to Neutral from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at Citi who warned that the agricultural giant’s planned spin-off of its seeds business and a challenging macro backdrop could weigh on performance through 2026.

Shares of Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) have already fallen about 9% since the company announced the separation, as investors questioned whether the move will truly unlock additional value. Citi analyst Patrick Cunningham said the spin-off may offer opportunities for growth and optimization but ultimately “adds more questions than answers” given a subdued farm economy and continued pricing pressure in crop protection.

Citi now values Corteva (CTVA) at 13 times 2026 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization for the seeds business and 10 times for the crop protection unit, setting a target price of $67 — down $16 from its prior view. That implies a multiple of 12.1 times versus 14 times previously.

“The ag macro setup into 2026 looks challenging,” Cunningham wrote in the Oct. 2 report to clients, citing weaker grain prices, thinner farmer margins and potential earnings revisions.

The firm highlighted uncertainty around potential dis-synergies, transaction costs and strategic execution as added risks following the separation. At the same time, Citi economists pointed to rising financial stress among farmers and muted export demand, noting that ongoing trade tensions with China and swelling soybean stockpiles could further pressure U.S. agricultural markets.

While soybean futures have recently rallied to around $11/bu, Citi cut its 2025/26 forecast to $4.30/bu, down from $4.50. Analysts said oversupply concerns and lackluster global demand remain headwinds despite recent U.S. government efforts to protect domestic producers.

Corteva’s fiscal 2026 ebitda forecast sits at about $4.1 billion, in line with consensus, but Citi warned that mounting cost pressures and pricing weakness could trigger downward revisions to both 2026 and 2027 earnings targets.

data.title: Robinhood's Tenev says tokenization will 'eat' the financial system,
data.description:
"Tokenization is a freight train, and it will eat the entire financial system," said Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) Chairman and CEO Vlad Tenev. But that doesn't mean it will crush traditional financial players.

"I think the financial system will be largely willing to go through this transition," he said in a televised interview on Bloomberg Television, aired on Thursday. 

In June, Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) introduced tokenized stocks in the EU, offering more than 200 digital versions of U.S. company shares and ETFs. It also launched and demonstrated tokenization of private companies OpenAI and SpaceX. Tokenized stocks are on-chain versions of the U.S. counterpart.

He expects the transformation to pick up speed outside the U.S. first, where there's a little bit less legacy infrastructure." The big existing players can consider it net new business, and "then it'll be somewhat back-ported," Tenev said.

Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) stock rose 2.1% in Thursday morning trading.

Once Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) introduced the tokenized securities, it started getting calls from many companies wanting to know how to get involved, including from "large established counterparties, market makers, institutions." They wanted to know if they could provide liquidity and if they could plug into HOOD's European offering. 

"I'm very optimistic that this is going to be a big shift, and we're at the very beginning of it," Tenev said.

He envisions that tokenization will encompass "all sorts of real-world assets," including art and real estate.

"All of these assets that have been typically been the domain of high net worth individuals — they've been illiquid. They've had high fees for transactions," he said. Through tokenization, they can be packaged and made available to the mass market.

He sees real estate as a big opportunity. "We've figured out how to do it," Tenev said. "It's just a question of regulation and what the right products are for different groups of customers."

Tenev doesn't feel threatened by major financial players adopting the technology. "I think with Nasdaq (NDAQ) and the other traditional players, I expect them to be a late adopter of the technology," he said. The main reason is that the traditional financial companies have lots of counterparties and customers that may not be able to move as quickly into the technology. 

Technology isn't the obstacle in realizing the vision. "Regulation is critical," he said. "A lot of regulators we talk to are very, very keen to make this happen. They recognize that tokenization is an innovation."

Tenev also discussed the potential for prediction markets to drive transformation across several existing industries.

"Prediction markets are a new source of information about real events, the government shutdown being top of mind today," Tenev said. "I think it just illustrates the power of this technology — not just as an investment asset, but as a source of news and information — almost like a new media."

Predication markets, as a sector, have the properties of trading, of gambling and of news. "It has the potential to disrupt each of these three major industries," he said.

In addition, prediction markets could be a huge accelerant outside the U.S. for the crypto ecosystem, Tenev added.

data.title: AngioDynamics raises sales outlook to $313M for FY26 as Med Tech segment accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AngioDynamics, Inc. (ANGO) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO James Clemmer highlighted that "we continue to grow across all areas of our business and performed especially well in the med tech markets that are critical to our future." He emphasized the company's shift to large, fast-growing markets, noting, "In Q1, we grew our revenue by 12%, led by the continued strength of our Med Tech segment, which grew 26%, marking our fourth consecutive quarter with over 20% growth."
  • Clemmer stated, "Our Auryon business has delivered another exceptional quarter, which continues to grow well above market rates... We are growing by taking share from all competitors in this space." He also pointed to strong performance in Mechanical Thrombectomy, with the business growing "over 40% versus the previous year."
  • Clemmer described growth in the NanoKnife business, attributing it to expanded prostate indication and preparation for a new CPT I code taking effect January 1. The company is launching an AARP campaign to increase awareness.
  • Clemmer concluded, "Our Med Tech business is accelerating. We're taking share with our superior technology, and we're driving sustained profitability."
  • CFO Stephen Trowbridge reported, "Revenue increased 12.2% to $75.7 million, driven by growth across both our Med Tech and Med Device segments. Med Tech revenue was $35.3 million, a 26.1% increase and our Med Device revenue was $40.4 million, an increase of 2.3%."
  • Trowbridge added, "Gross margin for the first quarter of FY '26 was 55.3%, a 90 basis point increase from the first quarter of FY '25. Primary drivers of the gross margin improvement are pricing initiatives in both our Med Tech and Med Device segments, the sales mix shift to our higher-margin Med Tech products and operating efficiencies."

Outlook

  • Trowbridge announced, "Based on our first quarter performance and our expectations for the balance of the year, we now expect net sales to be in the range of $308 million to $313 million, raised from our previously issued range of $305 million to $310 million."
  • Med Tech net sales are now expected to grow 14% to 16%, up from the prior guidance of 12% to 15%. Med Device sales are expected to remain roughly flat.
  • Trowbridge maintained full-year gross margin guidance at 53.5% to 55.5%, inclusive of $4 million to $6 million of tariff impact.
  • Full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance was increased to $6 million to $10 million from the previous $3 million to $8 million range. Adjusted loss per share is now expected to be between ($0.33) and ($0.23), an improvement from the prior range of ($0.35) to ($0.25).

Financial Results

  • Company revenue for the quarter was $75.7 million, with Med Tech revenue at $35.3 million and Med Device revenue at $40.4 million.
  • The Auryon platform contributed $16.5 million, growing 20.1% compared to last year. Mechanical Thrombectomy revenue reached $11.3 million, up 41.2% year-over-year. AngioVac revenue was $8 million, a 37.1% increase, while AlphaVac revenue was $3.3 million, up 52.3%. NanoKnife revenue was $6.4 million, an increase of 26.7%.
  • Gross margin improved to 55.3%, with Q1 including $1.7 million in tariff expense.
  • Adjusted net loss for the quarter was ($4.2 million) or ($0.10) per share, compared to ($4.4 million) or ($0.11) per share last year. Adjusted EBITDA was $2.2 million, compared to a loss of $152,000 in the prior year period.
  • Cash at quarter-end was $38.8 million, with expectations to be cash flow positive for the full year.

Q&A

  • John Young, Canaccord Genuity: Asked if raised Med Tech guidance was primarily due to Mechanical Thrombectomy and NanoKnife segments, and about growth cadence. Trowbridge: "Yes, you're correct... it is primarily being driven by what we're seeing in the Mechanical Thrombectomy and NanoKnife spaces."
  • Young inquired about NanoKnife disposable revenue and drivers. Trowbridge: Growth is "being driven by our prostate initiative... By and large, the growth that we're seeing there is all coming from prostate and the increased awareness."
  • Frank Takkinen, Lake Street Capital Markets: Sought update on hospital penetration for Mechanical Thrombectomy. Clemmer: "We've seen really good uptake in interest at the hospital and that translates into our sales team then converting accounts into the value analysis committee approvals that we seek."
  • Takkinen asked about sales force expansion. Clemmer: "We have now 50 people, 50 territories, identified who are just solely focused on AlphaVac and AngioVac."
  • Eduardo Martinez-Montes, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about price increases in thrombectomy revenue. Trowbridge: "We are seeing the ability to take some price in mechanical thrombectomy and we're doing that. But we're also bringing on new customers and we're seeing growth in terms of unit sales as well as utilization."
  • Martinez-Montes inquired about clinical trials. Trowbridge: "We're very pleased with the pace that we're seeing, where we're seeing enrollment in both the -- both sides of that trial and the RCT and the Registry."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment, congratulating management and focusing on growth in key segments, hospital penetration, and sales force effectiveness.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases like "we are bullish" and "we're positioned to deliver significant value creation for our shareholders."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts and management maintained a constructive dialogue, with a continued focus on growth drivers and operational execution. The tone remains positive, with management reinforcing confidence through improved guidance and detailed responses.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company increased its net sales outlook for FY26, with top guidance now at $313 million, compared to the previous quarter's $310 million.
  • Med Tech segment growth expectations were raised, while Med Device guidance remains flat.
  • Gross margin guidance is unchanged, but management highlighted earlier-than-expected benefits from operational initiatives.
  • Analyst focus this quarter shifted slightly more toward sales force expansion, hospital penetration, and the impact of new product indications (prostate for NanoKnife).
  • Management's confidence is reinforced by improved performance in the Med Tech segment and higher guidance, with more detailed discussion of pricing, utilization, and international expansion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management discussed the impact of tariffs, noting $1.7 million in Q1 expenses and ongoing expectations for $4 million to $6 million for the year.
  • Operating expenses remain a focus, with management emphasizing ongoing cost controls and operational leverage.
  • No additional divestitures or acquisitions were discussed, and management reiterated commitment to current portfolio and market execution.

Final Takeaway

AngioDynamics' management stressed its strong start to fiscal 2026, with robust Med Tech growth, improved margins, and a raised sales outlook. Key drivers include accelerated growth in Mechanical Thrombectomy and NanoKnife, successful sales force expansion, and positive reception for Auryon. Management is confident in the company's strategic direction, operational execution, and ability to drive sustained value for shareholders throughout the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: FCC Chairman Carr to testify before Senate following Kimmel incident - Reuters
data.description:
Federal Communications Commission Chairman Brendan Carr will testify before the Senate Commerce Committee in the wake of Disney's (NYSE:DIS) decision to suspend and then reinstate Jimmy Kimmel's show over the comedian's comments on slain right-wing activist Charlie Kirk, Reuters reported late on Wednesday, citing a source.

The report said Carr agreed to testify after speaking to Senate Commerce Committee Chairman Ted Cruz this week. The committee has jurisdiction over the FCC, and Democrats have pressed Cruz to call Carr to testify.

The source told Reuters that the date of the hearing has not been set but was expected after November. 

Cruz had condemned Carr for threatening to go after the licenses of Disney and local broadcasters who aired Kimmel's show.

"We can do this the easy way or the hard way. These companies can find ways to take action on Kimmel, or there is going to be additional work for the FCC ahead," Carr told media companies after Kimmel's comments.

Cruz said on a public podcast that Carr's comments were "dangerous as hell."

Kimmel's suspension lasted only for six days after advocates from both sides of the political aisle disagreed with the move. Disney also witnessed a wave of subscription cancellations by users who viewed the action against the comedian to be harsh and as an attack on free speech.

data.title: Alibaba's Amap, similar to Google Maps, hits record usage as Chinese holiday week begins
data.description:
Alibaba's (NYSE:BABA) Amap, which is similar to Google Maps (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL), reached a new high of 360M daily active users on Wednesday, the first day of China's "Golden Week" holiday, the company announced today.

Alibaba shares were up 4.6% during Thursday morning market action.

The start of the holiday, which is a peak travel and shopping period for China and coincides with National Day and the Mid-Autumn Festival, also featured the first wide-scale deployment of Amap's new artificial intelligence capabilities. Amap's AI assistant, Xiaogao, handled more than 2.6B user requests on October 1. 

Amap also released an AI-powered road safety alert system dubbed "Eagle Eye Guardian, that issued more than 290M alerts for "unusual driving behaviors." What's more, to help boost its engagement with businesses and drive commerce, it released Street Stars, an AI-powered ranking system for businesses. 

"To better support merchant partners, Amap also recently announced a one-year annual fee exemption for restaurants," Alibaba said. "This initiative and other support measures, like offline spending subsidies, have led to significant growth in the number of new restaurant merchants joining."   

In comparison, Google Maps reports it has more than 2B monthly active users and features more than 250M businesses and places of interest. Google Maps is currently blocked in mainland China, prompting locals and visitors to utilize Alibaba's Amap or Baidu Maps (BIDU).

The Golden Week holiday could provide a sales boost for Apple (AAPL), as China's consumer subsidies for iPhones remain in effect. The holiday was also increased this year to eight days instead of seven.

data.title: SA Charts: China EV market sees record September as NIO and XPeng hit new highs
data.description:
China’s EV market showed fresh signs of momentum in September 2025, with NIO and XPeng delivering their strongest monthly results to date. Li Auto staged a rebound from August’s weakness, while BYD maintained its lead in absolute volumes, though Y/Y growth softened.

Overall, September marked a turning point where emerging players gained significant traction, signaling intensifying rivalry in the world’s largest EV market.

Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI)

  • September Deliveries: 33,951
  • M/M Change: +19.0%
  • Y/Y Change: -36.8%

Li Auto saw a sharp rebound in September, with deliveries rising 19% from August’s slump. Still, volumes remain under pressure compared to last year, plunging nearly 37% Y/Y. The recovery signals near-term stabilization, but the broader downtrend shows demand headwinds are far from resolved.

XPeng (NYSE:XPEV)

  • September Deliveries: 41,581
  • M/M Change: +10.3%
  • Y/Y Change: +94.7%

XPeng extended its strong momentum with 41.6K units in September, a 10% sequential rise and nearly doubling Y/Y. While growth slowed slightly from earlier triple-digit surges, XPeng remains the standout among startups, consolidating market share with consistent gains across 2025.

NIO (NYSE:NIO)

  • September Deliveries: 34,749
  • M/M Change: +11.0%
  • Y/Y Change: +64.1%

NIO continued its recovery path, posting 34.7K deliveries, up 11% M/M. The 64% Y/Y surge underscores its turnaround momentum after a volatile first half of the year. Sustained double-digit sequential growth suggests its latest models are resonating strongly with buyers.

BYD (NYSE:BYD)

  • September Deliveries: 396,270
  • M/M Change: +6.1%
  • Y/Y Change: -5.5%

BYD remained the undisputed volume leader with nearly 400K units, though the 6% monthly uptick couldn’t offset a rare 6% Y/Y decline. With a high base effect and intensifying competition, BYD’s growth engine shows signs of cooling despite its dominant scale.

data.title: Amgen posts Phase 3 trial win for Repatha in major cardiovascular events
data.description:
Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) announced on Thursday that its PCSK9 inhibitor Repatha (evolocumab) reached the primary endpoints in a Phase 3 trial designed to evaluate its effect in reducing major adverse cardiovascular events such as stroke.

Citing data from its Phase 3 VESALIUS-CV clinical trial, the Thousand Oaks, California-based pharma giant said that Repatha reached the study’s dual primary endpoints, leading to a significant reduction in the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events.

The placebo-controlled global trial enrolled more than 12,000 adults with high-risk diabetes or established atherosclerotic disease who were on lipid-lowering medication but had no history of heart attack or stroke.

Recording a median follow-up of roughly 4.5 years, Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) said that VESALIUS-CV reached the main goals related to the first occurrence of a composite of heart disease events with a statistically and clinically significant impact. There were no new safety signals, the company added.

This year, the FDA broadened the Repatha label to include adults who are at a higher risk for major adverse cardiovascular events due to a high level of bad cholesterol known as LDL-C.

"Repatha is known as a highly effective LDL-C lowering treatment and is now the first and only PCSK9 inhibitor shown to reduce cardiovascular events in high-risk adults without prior heart attack or stroke,” said Amgen's (NASDAQ:AMGN) R&D chief, Jay Bradner.

data.title: Disney designates Hulu as global entertainment brand, launches new homepage features
data.description:
As part of the comprehensive integration of Disney (NYSE:DIS) with Hulu and ESPN, Hulu will become the global entertainment brand on Disney+, replacing Star in international markets beginning October 8.

The company is also making changes to its homepage to allow for simpler navigation across the Disney, Hulu, and ESPN streaming services, along with a “For You” tab that will provide curated programs based on viewing history.

The newly designed home page is more modern and intuitive, showcasing the latest titles from each brand.

Disney (NYSE:DIS) also plans to launch widgets on Apple (AAPL) iOS to take users directly to programming options. And in 2026, mobile-first and mobile-exclusive features will be launched to expand Disney’s reach, reduce churn, and engage new audiences.

“These enhancements are just the beginning, with additional updates planned in the lead-up to the launch of a unified app experience next year,” Disney said on their website.

data.title: National Grid says H1 earnings in line with expectations
data.description:
National Grid (NYSE:NGG) -0.9% in London trading Thursday after saying its H1 earnings are in line with its expectations and that it expects its underlying earnings per share to be weighted toward H2.

The British company, which did not provide financial figures detailing its expectations, plans to publish its half-year results on November 6.

National Grid (NYSE:NGG) said it expects operating profit to come in broadly consistent with previous periods, and profit from its U.K. Electricity Transmission and U.K. Electricity Distribution divisions to be broadly split across the year.

The company also anticipates H1 profit from its U.S. regulated businesses to rise due to fewer storms in New York and new electricity distribution rates in New England but remain weighted toward H2.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: RIVN, STLA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Thursday, with the S&P 500 reaching another record high, as investors largely brushed aside concerns about the ongoing U.S. government shutdown and continued to focus on positive momentum in equities.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Fair Isaac (FICO) +25% - Shares soared after the company unveiled its Mortgage Direct License Program, allowing mortgage tri-merge resellers to calculate and distribute FICO scores without relying on the three nationwide credit bureaus. The new model is expected to cut average per-score fees by up to 50%, setting pricing at $4.95 per score and a $33 funded loan fee per borrower, with lenders retaining the option to stick with the existing $10 per-score fee.
  • Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) +9% - Shares gained after the company posted impressive Q3 car sales numbers. FCA US reported Q3 U.S. sales of 324,825 vehicles, up 6% Y/Y, with growth across Jeep (+11%, led by Wrangler +18%, Gladiator +43%, and Wagoneer +122%), Ram (+26% retail sales driven by the HEMI-powered 1500), Chrysler (+45%, with Pacifica +49% and Voyager +65%), Dodge (Durango +44%, marking its best Q3 in 20 years alongside strong demand for the new SIXPACK-powered Charger lineup), and FIAT (+2%).
  • Bentley Systems (NASDAQ:BSY) +5% - Shares climbed after S&P Dow Jones Indices said the company will replace Western Union (WU) in the S&P MidCap 400, with WU moving to the S&P SmallCap 600 to take the place of Mr. Cooper Group (COOP), which was acquired by Rocket Companies (RKT) in a deal that closed October 1.

Biggest stock losers

  • Rivian (RIVN) -7% - Shares dipped after the company narrowed its 2025 delivery guidance and reported in-line Q3 delivery figures. Rivian produced 10,720 vehicles and delivered 13,201 in the third quarter, meeting forecasts. The company now expects 2025 deliveries of 41,500–43,500 vehicles, down from prior guidance of 40,000–46,000. 
  • Rocket Companies (NYSE:RKT) -5% - Shares slipped after the company filed a prospectus for the issuance of Class A common stock tied to the settlement of restricted stock units granted to former Mr. Cooper service providers under its Omnibus Incentive Plan.
  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY) -4% - Shares slid after Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) agreed to acquire OXY’s chemical business for $9.7B in cash, adding another non-insurance asset to Berkshire’s portfolio. Occidental will use proceeds to repay ~$6.5B in debt and strengthen its balance sheet, saving over $350M annually in interest expense. CEO Vicki Hollub said the deal enhances OXY’s financial flexibility, while Berkshire’s Greg Abel called the acquisition a strong fit, noting it will hardly dent Berkshire’s $344.1B cash pile.
